    Global Search Dialog
      1. Our Vision
      2. Focus Areas
      3. Truth, Repair and Transformation

      Truth, Repair and Transformation

      RWJF’s Truth, Repair and Transformation (TRT) process helps us address the root causes of health disparities by more explicitly focusing on truth-telling, repair, and accountability as necessary conditions for health equity and justice.

       Our TRT process systematically examines the origins of our wealth, our grantmaking impact, our internal culture, and the ways our practices have neglected or harmed communities most impacted by health inequities. By learning from our past, we can repair trust with communities and ensure we are better partners to grantees as we collectively work toward a future where health is a right for all.

      How RWJF Is Reckoning with Its History

      Being honest about our history means dismantling patterns that shape how we confront injustice.

      Read the blog post

      Truth, Repair and Transformation Wisdom Council

      An advisory group of 13 members provides expert guidance and consultation as RWJF works to dismantle structural racism as the biggest barrier to health. 

      Learn more about the Wisdom Council

      Related Content

      RWJF's Commitment to Equity and Justice

      RWJF is working toward a future where health is no longer a privilege, but a right for everyone—no matter who they are, where they live, or how much money they have.

      The Nowness of Reparations for Black People in New Jersey

      In 2023, the New Jersey Institute for Social Justice partnered with RWJF to convene the New Jersey Reparations Council. Through an intensive two-year study, the Council engaged to tell the story of New Jersey’s deep but often overlooked history of slavery and its enduring impact on the contemporary life of Black people in our home state.

      Read a product of the reparations study

      The Road to Health Equity and Justice is Hard, Worth It, and Best Traversed Together

      We have sometimes acted in ways that deepened the very inequities that we seek to undo. But we are listening, learning, and embracing new ways to do better.
      Health Disparities Structural Racism

      Understanding Our Past to Strengthen Our Commitment to Health Equity and Justice

      Transparency and accountability about RWJF’s historical legacy are key to the Foundation’s equity journey.
      Truth, Repair and Transformation Structural Racism
      Research

      Advancing Collective Wellbeing

      Collective wellbeing is both aspirational and operational. It is driven by a shared vision that generates actions to help people and communities not just survive, but thrive.
      Public and Community Health
      RWJF Annual Message

      Answering the Call for a Healthy, Inclusive Democracy

      During a time of calculated attacks against our democracy, we hear a call for justice from every neighborhood and community. A call for those with power, privilege, and resources to put those on the line for the betterment of everyone. And we will answer.
      Healthcare Coverage and Access Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Structural Racism
      Blog Post

      What Does a Just Future Look Like?

      People and communities across the country are upholding Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s legacy. Here’s how.
      Truth, Repair and Transformation Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

      Stay informed

      Sign up for the latest funding alerts, news, research, and perspectives from RWJF.

      Email address already subscribed

      Email address already subscribed. Please check your inbox to manage your subscriptions.

      Subscribed!

      Thank you. You are now subscribed.

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.

      Which profession or pursuit best describes you?
